extra.message: User input from 'name' parameter is inserted directly into SQL query using string formatting (% operator) instead of parameterized queries. The line VALUES ('%(name)s)' % {'name': name} allows SQL injection.
Jinja2 template engine is configured with autoescape=False, disabling automatic XSS protection. User-generated content rendered in templates will not be HTML-escaped, allowing XSS attacks.
CSRF middleware is commented out (# csrf_middleware), leaving the application without CSRF protection. All POST endpoints (login, students, courses, evaluate, review) are vulnerable to CSRF attacks.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Password hashing uses MD5 (md5(password.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()), which is cryptographically broken and vulnerable to collision attacks. MD5 is too fast and unsuitable for password hashing.

The students POST endpoint (line 54-57) allows creating students without any authentication or authorization check. The authorize() decorator is not applied, allowing unauthenticated users to add students.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The courses POST endpoint (line 86-89) allows creating courses without any authentication or authorization check. The authorize() decorator is not applied, allowing unauthenticated users to add courses.

extra.message: RedisStorage is initialized with httponly=False, allowing client-side JavaScript to access session cookies via document.cookie. This facilitates session hijacking through XSS attacks.

cwe: CWE-614: Sensitive Cookie in HTTPS Session Without 'HttpOnly' Flag
